Event: Ron's father drops Ron at the metal factory instead of college
Explanation: Saves himself perceived "waste" of college while severely burdening Ron, who must fend for himself.

Event: Ron hitchhikes his way to college
Explanation: Overcomes obstacle to reach college with no shown harm to others.

Event: Ron leaves 99% of Parks work to Leslie
Explanation: He protects his principles and comfort while shifting workload and bureaucratic drag onto Leslie and the public.

Event: Ron convinces Chris to undo office changes for coworkers' sake
Explanation: He sacrifices desired government collapse to restore coworkers’ morale and productivity.

Event: Ron eats extreme quantities of unhealthy food
Explanation: Extreme diet endangers his health without affecting others much.

Event: Jamm adopts Ron’s eating habits and gets sick
Explanation: He copies an unhealthy lifestyle that clearly harms him with no shown benefit.

Event: Ron hides his birthday from government records
Explanation: He preserves his privacy and comfort while mildly depriving others of social bonding.

Event: Ron intentionally sabotages Parks Department productivity
Explanation: He gains ideological satisfaction while reducing governmental effectiveness for citizens.

Event: Ron refuses to openly express caring for friends
Explanation: He maintains his emotional boundaries yet still supports them indirectly, modestly benefiting all.

Event: Ron retrieves escape backpack when Tammy One appears
Explanation: He protects himself from an abusive ex with no harm to others.

Event: Ron salts his gold caches with fake gold
Explanation: He improves his security by wasting thieves’ efforts and misleading them.

Event: Ron keeps secret whiskey in office wall for emergencies
Explanation: Prepares for stressful situations, helping himself without affecting others.

Event: Ron maintains 37 bacon caches anticipating a shortage
Explanation: He secures his own supply at effort and potential deprivation of shared resources.

Event: Ron lies to protect Donna after she rear-ends dealer
Explanation: He shields Donna from blame and financial loss while unfairly harming the dealer.

Event: Ron becomes a doormat around Tammy One
Explanation: He submits to control that harms his autonomy, mildly pleasing her.

Event: Ron grows into loving family man with Diane and children
Explanation: He gains deep familial fulfillment while providing care and stability.

Event: Ron gives Andy a scholarship
Explanation: He parts with money but gains satisfaction and helps Andy advance significantly.

Event: Ron loans money to Tom for his business
Explanation: He risks resources to support Tom’s entrepreneurial efforts and personal growth.

Event: Ron eats vegan bacon samples only to throw them out
Target: Others who might eat vegan bacon
Explanation: He amuses/protects himself from food he dislikes while wasting food others could have used.

Event: Ron forces boys’ basketball team to get short haircuts
Explanation: He enforces his aesthetic ideals at minor benefit to his sense of order and at cost to kids’ autonomy.

Event: Ron takes over Leslie’s duties in “Christmas Scandal”
Explanation: He steps in competently to keep government services functioning.

Event: Ron turns down Deputy City Manager job to stay in Parks
Explanation: He preserves a role he likes and stability for his team.

Event: Ron builds and crash-tests a crib with his car
Explanation: He risks his own car to ensure the crib’s safety for the child.

Event: Ron moonlights as Duke Silver
Explanation: He gains income and joy from music while entertaining his audience.

Event: Ron sells crafted flutes/recorders to doomsday cult
Explanation: He profits from his craft and provides valued instruments with no explicit harm in text.

Event: Ron steps up as Parental Substitute for April and Andy
Explanation: He invests time and care and significantly improves their lives and stability.

Event: Ron apologizes to April and asks her to return
Explanation: He repairs relationship and restores a valued employee, benefiting both.

Event: Ron lies to protect Donna and Joe relationship prospects
Explanation: He uses his judgment to push Donna away from a harmful pattern toward potential real happiness.

Event: Ron supports April and Andy’s relationship development
Explanation: He encourages a pairing that brings them happiness with no shown downside.

Event: Ron helps Ann learn home repair
Explanation: He spends effort teaching skills that empower Ann.

Event: Ron builds Chris a crib to ease his stress
Explanation: He uses his skill and time to materially reduce Chris’s anxiety.

Event: Ron protects Donna from “her Tammy” Joe
Explanation: He becomes suspicious on her behalf, steering her away from a toxic dynamic.

Event: Ron becomes friends with Typhoon and serves as best man
Explanation: He gains friendship and greatly honors/supports their wedding.

Event: Ron tries to slaughter a pig for a public park barbecue
Explanation: He pursues his ideal barbecue at odds with health and regulatory concerns.

Event: Ron walks out of barbecue when people complain about no kids’ drinks
Explanation: He preserves his principles but abandons hosting duties, inconveniencing others.

Event: Ron protects his son’s privacy and goes on offensive against Gryzzl
Explanation: He defends his child’s data rights, forcing a corporation to respect privacy.

Event: Ron stands protectively in front of his daughters around Tammy II
Explanation: He shields them from a dangerous ex, increasing their safety and his peace of mind.

Event: Ron goes to Scotland to visit Lagavulin distillery at Leslie’s urging
Explanation: He gains joy and perspective, and Leslie’s gesture is validated, strengthening bonds.

Event: Ron buys the Lagavulin distillery (controlling interest)
Explanation: He secures a profitable passion investment and likely stabilizes the business.

Event: Ron burns his Employee of the Month plaque and hides the ashes
Explanation: He reasserts his anti-government identity while rejecting colleagues’ recognition.
